title: Image Size, Build Performance, Runtime Efficiency, and Reproducibility · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Cache Performance Experiment
summary: Build time and image size doubled after dependency changes, while production startup and memory regress despite a warm local cache. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker cache performance experiment increment in measured build and runtime optimization experiment. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure cold, warm, changed-source, changed-lockfile, and remote-cache builds with plain progress and controlled inputs.
Measure compressed, unpacked, shared, unique, and writable size and trace which layer, package, or artifact owns the change.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.

version-baseline:

  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Docker Cache Performance Experiment · Theory Docker Cache Performance Experiment
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team compares a warm local build with a cold CI build and blames a large final image, while context transfer and cross-platform cache dominate. Measure each stage and compare controlled output digests.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Cache Performance Experiment contract for the supplied data.

For Predict Docker Cache Performance Experiment · Theory Docker Cache Performance Experiment, keep Docker Cache Performance Experiment connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: Measure cold, warm, changed-source, changed-lockfile, and remote-cache builds with plain progress and controlled inputs. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to Docker Cache Performance Experiment while producing measured build and runtime optimization experiment.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example team compares a warm local build with a cold CI build and blames a large final image, while context transfer and cross-platform cache dominate. Measure each stage and compare controlled output digests. Competency probe: Measure cold, warm, changed-source, changed-lockfile, and remote-cache builds with plain progress and controlled inputs. A defensible solution applies Docker Cache Performance Experiment, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to measured build and runtime optimization experiment.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-cache-performance-experiment — Measure cold, warm, changed-source, changed-lockfile, and remote-cache builds with plain progress and controlled inputs.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-performance-reproducibility-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-performance-reproducibility-3
    artifact: build-performance-budget.yaml
    decision: worked docker performance reproducibility 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      context-bytes: measured
      cache-hit-rate: observed-not-assumed
      cold-build: recorded
      image-bytes: per-platform
      runtime-budget: cpu-memory-io
      reproducibility: digest-compared
    verify: docker buildx du && docker image inspect candidate --format json
    failure: Reject image size as the sole performance metric, warm-cache timing as a cold build, or equal source commits as proof of equal image digests.
    repair: Measure context, transfer, layers, cache, startup, CPU, memory, and I/O separately; change one variable and compare digests under controlled inputs.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Reordering Dockerfile lines for more cache hits cannot alter correctness or trust. This fails the Docker Cache Performance Experiment contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure cold, warm, changed-source, changed-lockfile, and remote-cache builds with plain progress and controlled inputs.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-cache-performance-experiment-11wk99
  - competency: docker-cache-performance-experiment
    artifact: build-performance-budget.yaml
    decision: cache performance experiment
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: recovery host starting from an empty project namespace
    fault-injection: restore a volume under a different numeric user identity
    retained-proof: per-platform manifest digest and selected image configuration
    case-key: 11wk99
    change: |
      context-bytes: measured
      cache-hit-rate: observed-not-assumed
      cold-build: recorded
      image-bytes: per-platform
      runtime-budget: cpu-memory-io
      reproducibility: digest-compared
    verify: docker buildx du && docker image inspect candidate --format json
    failure: Reject image size as the sole performance metric, warm-cache timing as a cold build, or equal source commits as proof of equal image digests.
    repair: Measure context, transfer, layers, cache, startup, CPU, memory, and I/O separately; change one variable and compare digests under controlled inputs.
